import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ line: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  mut current = ""
  let n = string.len(line)
  mut i = 0
  while i < n
    match string.byte_at(line, i)
    when Some(c)
      if c == 32 or c == 9 or c == 13
        if not string.is_empty(current)
          match string.parse_int(current)
          when Some(v)
            result = list.push(result, v)
          when None
            ()
          end
          current = ""
        end
      else
        current = "#{current}#{string.from_byte(c)}"
      end
    when None
      ()
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if not string.is_empty(current)
    match string.parse_int(current)
    when Some(v)
      result = list.push(result, v)
    when None
      ()
    end
  end
  result
end

fn read_line_or_empty() -> String
  match io.read_line()
  when Some(s)
    s
  when None
    ""
  end
end

let line1 = read_line_or_empty()
let line2 = read_line_or_empty()

let nums = parse_ints(line1)

let threshold = match string.parse_int(string.trim(line2))
  when Some(v)
    v
  when None
    0
  end

mut filtered: List<Int> = []
for x in nums
  if x > threshold
    filtered = list.push(filtered, x)
  end
end

mut output = ""
mut first = true
for x in filtered
  if first
    output = "#{x}"
    first = false
  else
    output = "#{output} #{x}"
  end
end

println(output)
